channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Jamie Lee Curtis and Nicole Kidman Team Up for Scarpetta TV Series
item.description: Jamie Lee Curtis and Nicole Kidman have joined forces for 'Scarpetta', a new mystery crime series on Prime Video based on Patricia Cornwell's bestselling novels. Curtis, reflecting on her long Hollywo...

item.title: Overseas Filipino Workers Stranded in Saudi Arabia Repatriated via Singapore
item.description: Twenty-four overseas Filipino workers (OFWs) who were stranded in Saudi Arabia due to the escalating conflict in the Middle East have safely arrived in Singapore on a chartered flight. The Philippine ...

item.title: Digg restructures company, lays off staff, and discontinues app amid bot challenges
item.description: Digg, the link-sharing platform originally popularized by Kevin Rose, announced significant staff layoffs and the removal of its mobile app as part of a company-wide restructuring effort. Despite thes...

item.title: Teacher in Mara Region Receives Long-Delayed Transfer Allowances After Eight Years
item.description: After waiting for more than eight years, Samuel Magutu, a primary school teacher in Tarime District, Mara Region, has finally received Sh4.2 million in transfer allowances. The delay occurred when he ...

item.title: ADC Chairman Accuses FCT Senator Ireti Kingibe Over Missing ₦100 Million Campaign Fund
item.description: The Abuja Municipal Area Council (AMAC) chairman of the African Democratic Congress (ADC), Omale Sule, don accuse Federal Capital Territory (FCT) Senator, Ireti Kingibe, say she receive ₦100 million f...

item.title: Historical Lessons Could Have Increased Public Support for Trump’s Iran Actions
item.description: Public backing for the United States’ military actions in Iran is currently among the lowest in modern history. According to political analysts, much of this hesitation stems from a lack of clear expl...

item.title: CBE set to boost student capacity with new ten-storey facility
item.description: The College of Business Education (CBE) in Dar es Salaam is preparing to expand its student enrollment as its new ten-storey Weights and Measures building reaches 93 percent completion. This developme...

item.title: School Bus Driver Receives 60-Day Sentence After Fatal Pedestrian Collision
item.description: A 61-year-old school bus driver, Anthony Phillips, has been sentenced to 60 days in jail and a two-year driving suspension after pleading guilty to careless driving causing the death of 22-year-old Ra...

item.title: Nigerian Singer Pheelz Victim of Car Break-In Robbery in Los Angeles
item.description: Nigerian singer and music producer Pheelz, whose real name is Phillip Moses, recently experience one serious robbery incident for Los Angeles, United States, where him dey currently based. Him take to...

item.title: Khampepe Commission Continues Probe into TRC Political Interference
item.description: The Khampepe commission of inquiry, which is investigating allegations of political interference in Truth and Reconciliation Commission (TRC) cases, resumed in Johannesburg on Friday. Recent weeks hav...

item.title: Chancellor Reeves and Energy Secretary Miliband meet petrol retailers to address rising fuel prices amid Middle East conflict
item.description: Chancellor Rachel Reeves hosted a meeting at 11 Downing Street with representatives from the Petrol Retailers Association (PRA) and executives from major companies including Asda, BP, ExxonMobil, and ...

item.title: Kennedy Center President Richard Grenell to Step Down Before Renovation Closure
item.description: Richard Grenell, president of the Kennedy Center, is set to step down from his role, with Matt Floca, the centre's vice president of facilities operations, taking over as Chief Operating Officer and E...

item.title: Gabriel Ayimba Earns First Shujaa Call-Up for World 7s Division 2 Tourney in Uruguay
item.description: Gabriel Ayimba, a young and promising rugby talent, is set to make his debut for the Kenya Sevens team, Shujaa, during the second leg of the World Rugby 7s Division 2 tournament in Montevideo, Uruguay...

item.title: Procedure to Remove Election Commission Chief and Opposition’s Preparedness
item.description: The news article discusses the procedural framework for impeaching the Chief of the Election Commission of India and the stance of opposition parties regarding this process. It outlines the constituti...

item.title: Rising Costs of Chips in Australia: From Supermarkets to Fine Dining
item.description: In Australia, the humble side of chips is no longer as cheap as its old saying suggests. At high-end restaurants such as Infinity by Mark Best and Merivale's Mimi’s in Sydney, a serving of fries can c...

item.title: Onedosh App Offers Fast, Borderless Money Management for Everyone
item.description: OneDosh na new financial app wey dey promise to change how people dey manage their money for this 5G world. The app dey built to help people move money quick without wahala, anywhere for the world. E ...

item.title: Rainfall Expected in Nairobi, Highlands, Western Kenya, and Coastal Areas Over the Next Day
item.description: The Kenya Meteorological Department (KMD) has issued a weather advisory indicating that several counties across Kenya are likely to experience rainfall over the next 24 hours. Nairobi, along with coun...

item.title: Mamelodi Sundowns take 3-0 first-leg lead over Stade Malien in CAF Champions League quarterfinal
item.description: Mamelodi Sundowns delivered a commanding 3-0 victory over Stade Malien in the first leg of the CAF Champions League quarterfinals at Loftus Versfeld Stadium in Pretoria. Goals from Khuliso Mudau, Bray...

item.title: Kenya hosts World Rally Championship Safari Rally in Naivasha
item.description: Kenya is buzzing with excitement as the third leg of the 2026 World Rally Championship (WRC) takes place in Naivasha from March 12 to 15. The iconic Safari Rally features a total distance of 842.9 km,...

item.title: Igbinedion Education Centre Expels Students Over Brutal Bullying Incident in Benin City
item.description: Some students of Igbinedion Education Centre for Secondary School for Benin City don dey expelled because dem assault and bully one of their fellow student inside classroom. One video wey go viral sho...
